title: [$250] Messages not highlighted as unread in LHN on web / real-time message updates not reflected

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Needs Reproduction
labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem New messages in NewDot on web are sometimes not highlighted as unread in the LHN (Left Hand Navigation), causing users to silently miss incoming messages. Additionally, the LHN preview may show stale content — different from the latest message in the conversation — and the actual message only appears after navigating away from and back into the conversation. Reported on Staging web v9.3.70-3. Multiple users have independently confirmed the behavior. ## Current Behavior - A new message arrives in a conversation but the conversation is **not** marked as unread in the LHN - The user only discovers the new message by manually opening the conversation - The LHN row preview does not update to reflect the latest message in real time - The correct message eventually appears after navigating out of and back into the conversation ## Expected Behavior - New messages mark the conversation as unread in the LHN - The LHN row preview updates in real time to show the latest message - Users receive the appropriate unread badge/highlight so they are aware of new activity ## Platforms Confirmed - Web (Staging v9.3.70-3) ## Reproduction Steps No reliable steps to reproduce yet. The issue occurs intermittently — more often than not messages do appear as unread, but users have reported it failing across multiple separate conversations. ## Notes - Slack thread with reports: https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1778593328736269 - Related reports from carlos: https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1778602314138499 ## Who Can Help Reproduce? If you experience this, please share: 1. Platform and app version 2. Whether you are the sender or receiver of the message 3. Approximate time the message was sent 4. Whether the LHN preview was also stale or only the unread state was wrong 5. Any actions taken immediately before the missed unread (e.g. switching tabs, navigating away)

title: [$250] [Bug] Gusto approval mode shows "Not Set" for workspaces originally connected via Classic

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Bug For Gusto-connected workspaces that were originally connected via Classic (OldDot), the **Approval Mode** option in NewDot renders as "Not Set" even though the policy has an approval mode stored. Root cause: Classic stores the legacy enum value (`APPROVAL_SUBMIT_AND_APPROVE` / `APPROVAL_ADVANCED` / `APPROVAL_MANUAL`) in `policy.connections.gusto.config.approvalMode`, but [GustoApprovalModePage.tsx](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/workspace/hr/gusto/GustoApprovalModePage.tsx) compares this strictly against `CONST.GUSTO.APPROVAL_MODE` (`basic` / `manager` / `custom`), so no option ends up selected. A backend fix in Web-Expensify ([yuwen-fix-gusto-default-approval-mode](https://github.com/Expensify/Web-Expensify/tree/yuwen-fix-gusto-default-approval-mode)) seeds the NewDot alias on initial connect when the OAuth flow originates in NewDot. But that doesn't help workspaces that were originally connected via Classic — those still have legacy values stored, and the NewDot selection page needs to handle them. ## Steps to reproduce 1. Pick a workspace that connected Gusto via Classic (so `approvalMode` is one of `APPROVAL_SUBMIT_AND_APPROVE` / `APPROVAL_ADVANCED` / `APPROVAL_MANUAL`). 2. In NewDot, open Workspace → HR → Gusto → Approval Mode. 3. Observe that none of the options (Basic / Manager / Custom) are selected — the page shows "Not Set". ## Proposed fix Normalize the legacy values to NewDot aliases when computing `isSelected` (and likely also in the read site that shows the current selection on the parent page): | Stored value | NewDot equivalent | | --- | --- | | `APPROVAL_SUBMIT_AND_APPROVE` | `basic` | | `APPROVAL_ADVANCED` | `manager` | | `APPROVAL_MANUAL` | `custom` | Mapping mirrors [`HrIntegrationApprovalMode`](https://github.com/Expensify/Integration-Server/blob/main/src/expensify/beans/expensify/enums/HrIntegrationApprovalMode.java) on the Integration-Server side. This should be done as a read-only normalization on the client — do not write back the alias automatically (the backend PR handles that for new NewDot connects). Touching existing config on render could cause unintended pendingFields churn. ## Parent For https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/591910
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ShridharGoel

title: [$250] [Bug] Switching Gusto approval mode does not show a sync spinner immediately

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9dde9d46-f05d-464e-b866-2dfb4460c3b4 ## Bug When switching the Gusto approval mode in Workspace → HR (NewDot), the sync spinner doesn't appear immediately. There is a noticeable delay before any sync UI is shown, because the client waits for the first push-notification of sync progress from the backend before rendering the spinner. The spinner should be queued optimistically the moment the user toggles the approval mode (i.e. when the `UpdateGustoApprovalMode` request is fired), so it's clear a sync is happening — rather than waiting for the IS to push a progress update. ## Steps to reproduce 1. On a Control workspace, enable the HR feature and connect Gusto. 2. Open the Gusto approval mode page. 3. Switch between approval modes (e.g. Basic ↔ Manager). 4. Observe that nothing indicates a sync is running for a beat or two; the spinner only shows up once the first push notification of sync progress is received. ## Expected behavior Toggling the approval mode should immediately put the connection into a syncing/spinner state in the UI (optimistic update), then transition into the real progress updates as they come in over push notifications. ## Parent For https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/591910
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ShridharGoel

title: [$250] Sage Intacct export links incorrectly show QBO URL in report history

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
**Version Number:** N/A (present on current production) **Reproducible in staging?:** Unknown **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/635106 **Issue reported by:** Melvin **Slack conversation:** ## Action Performed: 1. Have a workspace connected to Sage Intacct 2. Create and submit a report with non-reimbursable expenses 3. Approve and export the report to Sage Intacct 4. View the export history in the report chat (NewDot) ## Expected Result: The export history message should either show a valid Sage Intacct link or no link (since Intacct exports store IDs, not URLs). ## Actual Result: The export history message shows a link to QuickBooks Online (`QBO_EXPENSES_URL`) instead of Sage Intacct. ## Root Cause In [`src/libs/ReportActionsUtils.ts:2732-2746`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d711875aba0b1a929bed6b2d3aeea795921e78c6/src/libs/ReportActionsUtils.ts#L2732-L2746), when a report has multiple non-reimbursable export entries, a `switch(label)` determines the export URL. There is **no case for Intacct**, so the `default` case returns `QBO_EXPENSES_URL`. Additionally, there is a label mismatch: Integration Server sends `'Intacct'` as the label, but `CONST.POLICY.CONNECTIONS.NAME_USER_FRIENDLY.intacct` is `'Sage Intacct'`. Even if a case were added using the App constant, it wouldn't match the label from the server. **Note:** OldDot handles this correctly — it explicitly returns no links for Intacct/QBD since these fields contain IDs, not URLs. The single-entry path in NewDot also handles it correctly via a `startsWith('https://')` check at line 2730, but the multi-entry path (line 2731+) enters the switch and hits the wrong default. ## Recommended Fix Add a guard before the switch statement (or an explicit case) to return no link URL for Intacct labels — checking for both `'Intacct'` and `'Sage Intacct'`. ## Workaround: View the export in OldDot, which renders correctly. ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari (This is a data-rendering bug in shared JS code — affects all platforms.) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: Standardize Expense Report and Expense header with predictable button placement and carousel counter

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

body: We have mixed styles for showing the carousel counter on the expense report header as well as the expense header. Sometimes the carousel counter has a "X of X" label, though it doesn't on expenses. We also don't always show the expense carousel counter when you view expenses from the Spend page - we only show it when you view an expense from within an expense report. We also show the carousel counter buttons on the same line as the next steps, which feels counter intuitive given that the carousel counter is more of a parent item not attached to next steps. We'd like to standardize on the buttons here across the two types: expense reports and expenses: Notable changes: - Let's move the report buttons to be down near the report next steps, on the same vertical line - Let's always have the carousel buttons in the top right - Let's make sure we always show the carousel for expenses when you access expenses from the Spend page, the same way you see a carousel when accessing Reports from the Reports page Once we have this framework in place, we'll be able to do things like launch a violation fixer from Home that will always have a consistent carousel button placement. cc @JmillsExpensify @trjExpensify @dannymcclain @dubielzyk-expensify
